_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to develop and evaluate the quality of questions that guide our investigation of contemporary political, legal, and civic issues.

_source.w: We are learning to develop and evaluate the quality of questions that guide our investigation of contemporary political, legal, and civic issues.
y: The quality of our questions determines the quality of our investigation — evaluating them is a metacognitive skill that improves all our work.
Sophisticated questions address complexity, consider multiple perspectives, and lead to nuanced conclusions.
This self-evaluative practice builds intellectual rigour and academic maturity.
eg: Developing a multi-layered question about a contemporary political issue that addresses causes, consequences, and perspectives.
Evaluating our own questions against criteria — Are they specific? Researchable? Analytically productive?
Revising questions based on initial research findings to sharpen our investigation focus.
Comparing our questions with published research questions and identifying what makes them effective.
Peer-reviewing classmates' questions and providing constructive feedback.
Reflecting on how the quality of our questions improved throughout the investigation.
